name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Castes (National)
provider: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
source_name: National Scholarship Portal — Post-Matric Scholarship for SC
summary: Post-matric scholarship for Scheduled Caste students pursuing education at Class 11 level and above, with family income up to ₹2.5 lakh per annum. Centrally sponsored and distributed via states/UTs through the National Scholarship Portal.
source_note: Centrally sponsored scheme administered via NSP. States disburse funds; exact amounts and additional eligibility conditions vary by state.
verification_note: No minimum marks requirement at the national level — min_marks set to null. Income limit of ₹2.5 lakh is the standard NSP-listed ceiling for this scheme. Deadline is approximate (typically October–November); verify current cycle on NSP. State-specific sub-conditions (e.g. Maharashtra may require 50% marks) cannot be captured in a single national record.

name: National Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities (Post-Matric)
provider: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
source_name: National Scholarship Portal — DEPwD Post-Matric Scholarship for PwD
summary: Post-matric scholarship exclusively for students with benchmark disabilities (government-issued certificate of 40% or above), to support education at Class 11 level and above. Income limit ₹2.5 lakh/year.
source_note: DEPwD scheme distributed via NSP. Three sub-categories exist (pre-matric, post-matric, top-class); this record covers post-matric. Corresponds to mock entry sc-003.
verification_note: No minimum academic marks criterion for this scheme — min_marks is null. The figure 40% refers exclusively to the disability certificate threshold (benchmark disability ≥40% as per the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016), which is captured by disability_requirement: required and the docs_required list; it must NOT be interpreted as an academic score floor. Income limit of ₹2.5 lakh per annum (family) is well-established in NSP guidelines. Deadline is approximate (typically October–November); verify on NSP. Document list based on standard NSP requirements — exact docs may have changed in recent cycles. Top-class category for PwD students at premier institutions is a separate higher-value scheme.
